meals.name: Sheet Pan Teriyaki Chicken & Broccoli with Rice
meals.description: An easy and delicious Asian-inspired sheet pan dinner with tender chicken and crisp-tender broccoli, served over fluffy white rice. Total cook time ~40 minutes.

ingredients.name: Boneless, skinless chicken breasts
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Tyson® All Natural Fresh Boneless Skinless Chicken Breasts with Rib Meat

ingredients.name: White rice, uncooked
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Mahatma Extra Long Grain White Rice, Uncooked Rice, 80 oz (2.27kg)

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Prepare rice according to package directions. In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, grated ginger, minced garlic, and sesame oil to make the teriyaki sauce.
Step 2: Cut chicken breasts into 1-inch pieces. On a large baking sheet, toss the chicken and broccoli florets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Spread them in a single layer.
Step 3: Drizzle half of the teriyaki sauce over the chicken and broccoli, tossing to coat. Bake for 15 minutes.

Step 4: Remove the baking sheet from the oven, toss the chicken and broccoli, and drizzle with the remaining teriyaki sauce. Return to the oven and bake for another 8-10 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through (internal temperature 165°F / 74°C) and broccoli is tender-crisp.
Step 5: Serve the teriyaki chicken and broccoli immediately over the cooked white rice, garnished with sesame seeds if desired.

meals.name: Quick Beef & Black Bean Tacos
meals.description: Fast and flavorful ground beef tacos with black beans, ready in under 30 minutes, topped with your favorite fresh ingredients. Total cook time ~25 minutes.

ingredients.name: Canned black beans, rinsed and drained
Soft taco shells or hard taco shells
Shredded lettuce
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Iceberg Shredded Lettuce Bagged Salad BIG DEAL!

ingredients.name: Shredded cheddar or Mexican blend cheese

instructions: Step 1: In a large skillet, brown the ground beef over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess grease.
Step 2: Add the taco seasoning mix and 1/4 cup of water to the skillet. Stir well and bring to a simmer. Stir in the rinsed and drained black beans.
Step 3: Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the beans are heated through.

Step 4: Warm the taco shells according to package directions. Serve the beef and black bean mixture in the warmed taco shells, topped with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, shredded cheese, sour cream, and salsa.

meals.name: Slow Cooker BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwiches with Coleslaw
meals.description: Tender, slow-cooked pulled pork simmered in a tangy BBQ sauce, perfect for sandwiches, served with a fresh and creamy coleslaw. Total cook time ~8 hours (passive).

ingredients.name: Boneless pork shoulder (pork butt)
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Boneless Pork Shoulder Butt Carnitas

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Light Brown Sugar

ingredients.name: Apple cider vinegar
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic™ Organic Raw Apple Cider Vinegar

ingredients.name: Hamburger buns
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Rich N Honey Hamburger Buns

instructions: Step 1: Place the chopped yellow onion at the bottom of a 6-quart slow cooker. Season the pork shoulder liberally with salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, chili powder, and cumin. Place the seasoned pork on top of the onions.
Step 2: In a bowl, whisk together the brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, and liquid smoke (if using) to create the BBQ sauce. Pour the sauce over the pork.
Step 3: Cover and cook on LOW for 6-8 hours, or on HIGH for 3-4 hours, until the pork is fork-tender and easily shreds.

Step 4: Carefully remove the pork from the slow cooker and place it on a cutting board. Shred the pork using two forks, discarding any large pieces of fat. Return the shredded pork to the slow cooker and stir to coat it in the sauce.

Step 5: For the coleslaw, combine the coleslaw mix, mayonnaise, white vinegar, sugar, salt, and pepper in a medium bowl. Stir until well combined. Serve the pulled pork on hamburger buns with a side of coleslaw.

meals.name: Beef & Broccoli Rice Bowls
meals.description: A quick and satisfying skillet meal featuring tender beef and crisp broccoli tossed in a delicious soy-ginger sauce, served over fluffy white rice. Total cook time ~35 minutes.

ingredients.name: Flank steak, thinly sliced against the grain
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Choice Boneless Angus Beef Flank Steak

ingredients.name: White rice, uncooked
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Mahatma Extra Long Grain White Rice, Uncooked Rice, 80 oz (2.27kg)

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Light Brown Sugar

instructions: Step 1: Prepare rice according to package directions. In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, beef broth, cornstarch, grated ginger, minced garlic, sesame oil, and brown sugar to make the sauce.

Step 2: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the thinly sliced flank steak and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned. Remove the beef from the skillet and set aside.

Step 3: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the skillet. Add the broccoli florets and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. You can add a splash of water and cover for a few minutes to steam if needed.

Step 4: Return the cooked beef to the skillet with the broccoli. Pour the sauce over the beef and broccoli, stirring constantly, and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the ingredients.
Step 5: Serve the beef and broccoli mixture immediately over the cooked white rice, garnished with sesame seeds if desired.

meals.name: Quick Shrimp Scampi with Linguine
meals.description: Succulent shrimp sautéed in a bright garlic-lemon butter sauce, served over linguine for a restaurant-quality meal in under 30 minutes. Total cook time ~20 minutes.

ingredients.name: Raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Extra Large Raw Shrimp Peeled and Deveined Tall Off

ingredients.name: Lemon, juiced and zested
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Seventh Generation Lemon Zest Scent Multi-Surface Wipes

ingredients.name: Dry white wine (optional)
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Cook's California Champagne Extra Dry White Sparkling Wine

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley, chopped
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Gourmet Garden Lightly Dried Chopped Parsley

instructions: Step 1: Cook linguine according to package directions in a large pot of salted water. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta water before draining.

Step 2: While pasta cooks, pat shrimp dry and season with salt and pepper. In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter with 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and crushed red pepper flakes; sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Step 3: Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and opaque. Remove shrimp from the skillet and set aside.
Step 4: Deglaze the skillet with white wine (if using) or chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits. Add lemon juice, lemon zest, and remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Bring to a simmer.

Step 5: Add the drained linguine and cooked shrimp back to the skillet. Toss to coat in the sauce, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to reach desired consistency. Stir in fresh parsley and serve immediately.

meals.name: Crispy Baked Chicken Drumsticks with Roasted Sweet Potatoes & Onions
meals.description: Flavorful chicken drumsticks baked until crispy, served alongside sweet and savory roasted sweet potatoes and onions. Total cook time ~65 minutes.

ingredients.name: Sweet potatoes, peeled and diced into 1-inch cubes

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). In a shallow dish, combine beaten eggs and milk. In another shallow dish, mix panko bread crumbs with paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.

Step 2: Pat chicken drumsticks dry. Dip each drumstick first in the egg wash, then dredge thoroughly in the panko mixture, pressing to coat. Place coated drumsticks on a large baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a wire rack.

Step 3: In a separate bowl, toss the diced sweet potatoes and onion wedges with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them in a single layer on a second baking sheet (or around the chicken if there's enough space).

Step 4: Bake chicken and vegetables for 25 minutes. Flip the drumsticks and toss the vegetables. Continue baking for another 20-25 minutes, or until chicken is golden brown, crispy, cooked through (internal temperature 165°F / 74°C), and vegetables are tender.

meals.name: Baked Salmon with Lemon-Herb Roasted Asparagus and Potatoes
meals.description: Flaky baked salmon fillets paired with tender, flavorful asparagus and potatoes roasted with lemon and herbs for a healthy and satisfying meal. Total cook time ~50 minutes.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillets
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Great American Seafood Farm Raised Atlantic Salmon Fillets

ingredients.name: Lemon, sliced
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Bakery Fresh Lemon Sliced Loaf Cake

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). On a large baking sheet, toss the diced potatoes with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, garlic powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Spread in a single layer.
Step 2: Roast the potatoes for 20 minutes. While potatoes roast, pat salmon fillets dry and season with salt and pepper. In a bowl, toss asparagus with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper.

Step 3: After 20 minutes, remove the baking sheet from the oven. Push the potatoes to one side. Add the asparagus and salmon fillets to the baking sheet. Top each salmon fillet with a lemon slice and sprinkle with fresh dill.

Step 4: Return the baking sheet to the oven and bake for another 15-20 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork (internal temperature 145°F / 63°C) and asparagus is tender-crisp. Serve immediately.
